message:
refactor(http): revert CRLF scanning to windows()-based implementation

Reverts the dedicated find_headers_end() and find_crlf() byte-scanning
functions back to the idiomatic buf.windows(N).position() pattern.

The previous commit (7c2c243) introduced manual byte-by-byte loops to avoid
the iterator overhead of windows(). However, LLVM already optimizes the
windows(2)/windows(4) + PartialEq pattern into equivalent byte comparisons
in release builds, making the manual loops redundant complexity without
measurable throughput improvement.

Changes:
- find_headers_end(): restores one-liner windows(4) implementation
- Removes find_crlf() helper entirely
- split_line_crlf(): restores inline windows(2) call
- decode_head(): restores 3 inline windows(2) calls for request-line
  length check, request-line extraction, and header line iteration
- Header parsing loop reverts to relative offset arithmetic
  (cursor + line_end) from the absolute-index approach

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
